items.title: ‘Hands Off Our NHS’: Anti-Palantir Protests Break Out in UK Over Deal With National Health Service
items.summary: Crowding the gates of a major health care conference, protesters called for Palantir to be booted out of the UK’s National Health Service over privacy concerns and political grievances.

items.title: Meet the OpenAI Engineer Leading ChatGPT’s Biggest Transformation Yet
items.summary: Thibault Sottiaux helped make AI coding one of OpenAI’s fastest-growing businesses. Now he’s overseeing a sweeping overhaul of ChatGPT.

items.title: Massive Effigy of Elon Musk Raised Over Times Square to Protest Grok
items.summary: Activists raised a 40-foot-tall inflatable Elon Musk in Manhattan to draw attention to the risk he allegedly poses to investors.

items.title: SpaceX officially prices shares at $135 in the largest IPO ever
items.summary: Wits its official share pricing announcement, SpaceX's IPO has begun.

items.title: Oracle warns of security bug that hackers abused to breach 100+ companies
items.summary: The tech giant warned of a security flaw that a cybercrime gang said it's exploiting as part of a mass-hacking campaign. Google said it notified more than 100 organizations that had potentially vulnerable servers.

items.title: SpaceX SPV investors won’t know their true holdings until post-IPO lock-ups lift
items.summary: After SpaceX makes its public debut, lower-tier SPV investors face hidden fees, lengthy payout delays, and the risk of outright fraud.

items.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
items.summary: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

items.title: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
items.summary: Rights groups accuse the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in el-Obeid, a city on the civil war's front line.

items.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, performers and vibrant fans
items.summary: A star-studded opening ceremony featured artists from the World Cup's official soundtrack.

items.title: SpaceX cuts retail IPO allocation to low 20% range, source says
items.summary: The Elon Musk-led company plans to direct a percentage in the low 20s of the offering to retail buyers, according to a person familiar with the matter.

items.title: SpaceX to close above $2 trillion market cap on its debut, prediction market traders say
items.summary: Traders on Polymarket think it's likely SpaceX will join a group of just six companies with a market value beyond $2 trillion.

items.title: From startup to $1.8 trillion: The investors who took a chance on SpaceX now reap the rewards
items.summary: With SpaceX seeking an IPO valuation of nearly $1.8 trillion, early bets are poised to generate some of the biggest paper gains in venture capital history.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Senate defense policy bill excludes IVF provision
items.summary: The Senate Armed Services Committee rejected a provision in the annual defense policy bill that would have expanded coverage of fertility services for military members and their families, including in vitro fertilization, according to Sen. Tammy Duckworth (D-Ill.). “All my provision seeks to do is provide our troops with the same access to IVF that all Members of Congress already have. After everything our…

items.title: Judge rules Ken Paxton cannot move forward with ActBlue lawsuit
items.summary: A federal judge in Massachusetts ruled Thursday that Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton (R) cannot move forward with a donation-vetting lawsuit against progressive fundraising platform ActBlue. District Judge Richard G. Stearns blocked Paxton from “continuing to litigate” the case, finding that ActBlue was likely to succeed in its claims that the action infringed on its…

items.title: Kaitlan Collins: MAGA base doesn’t realize Trump ‘seeks validation’ from mainstream media
items.summary: CNN’s Kaitlan Collins said Tuesday in an interview that President Trump’s core base of supporters do not know that he “seeks validation” from the news media, despite his longstanding feud with the news media. The CNN reporter spoke with New York Times reporter Shawn McCreesh for Interview magazine about the president’s relationship with the media. Collins…

items.title: Scientists discover a strange property in rice and turn it into a smart material
items.summary: Scientists discovered that rice behaves in a highly unusual way: it weakens under rapid compression but stays stronger when pressure is applied slowly. Using this effect, they engineered a new material that reacts differently to gentle movements and sudden impacts. The material can adapt its stiffness automatically, opening the door to safer soft robots and protective equipment that responds instantly to collisions.

items.title: The deadly tapeworm spreading across America has reached the Pacific Northwest
items.summary: A potentially dangerous tapeworm linked to severe, cancer-like disease has now been found in the Pacific Northwest, marking its first detection in wild animals along the U.S. West Coast. Researchers discovered the parasite, Echinococcus multilocularis, in 37% of coyotes tested around Puget Sound—a surprisingly high rate for a region where it had never been reported until recently.

items.title: NASA reveals Artemis III crew for one of the most complex space missions ever
items.summary: NASA has selected the Artemis III crew for a high-stakes 2027 mission designed to test the future of lunar exploration. Astronauts will launch aboard Orion and perform unprecedented docking operations with lunar landers being developed by both Blue Origin and SpaceX. The mission will require a remarkable sequence of heavy-lift rocket launches and complex in-space maneuvers, helping pave the way for future Moon landings and eventually crewed missions to Mars.

items.title: Songs prep the brains of finches yet to hatch for a hot world
items.summary: Adult finches make "heat calls" as the temperature rises. Exposure to the song prepares their unhatched young's brains for the heat.

items.title: Measles has no treatments. Getting some may not be easy
items.summary: Vaccination remains the priority, but some researchers are looking for drugs to fight the virus in people who don't get the shot.

items.title: Clocks made from an atomic nucleus just ticked on for the first time
items.summary: First dreamed up decades ago, the world's first nuclear clocks are set to improve quickly, becoming more precise and aiding the hunt for dark matter.
